title: T-116 — OpenTelemetry baseline package + Next.js instrumentation.ts + worker init (P0 observability)
prompt:
# T-116 — OpenTelemetry baseline

PRD: docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md §5 Cross-cutting infra ("Observability: Sentry + Datadog / OpenTelemetry — MES-TRENDS-2026 §9"), §13 Niefunkcjonalne (uptime, page-load P95)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create `packages/observability` (`@monopilot/observability`) exporting a configured OpenTelemetry **tracer + meter** backed by `@opentelemetry/sdk-node` for `apps/worker` and `@opentelemetry/sdk-trace-web` + Next.js `instrumentation.ts` for `apps/web`. Today every module assumes a logger/tracer exists; auditor B Top-10 Gap #3 ("No production observability scaffolding — zero foundation tasks mention OpenTelemetry") confirms there is nothing wired.

This task delivers the OTel **infrastructure**. Structured logger (pino) is T-117. Sentry is T-118. The three together form the observability triple.

## Implementation contract
1. Create `packages/observability/` workspace package. Deps: `@opentelemetry/api`, `@opentelemetry/sdk-node`, `@opentelemetry/sdk-trace-base`, `@opentelemetry/sdk-trace-node`, `@opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-http`, `@opentelemetry/resources`, `@opentelemetry/semantic-conventions`, `@opentelemetry/instrumentation-pg`, `@opentelemetry/instrumentation-http`. Dev: vitest.
2. `packages/observability/src/tracer.ts` exports `getTracer(name: string, version?: string)` returning `Tracer` from `@opentelemetry/api`. Default tracer name `@monopilot/${pkg}`.
3. `packages/observability/src/meter.ts` exports `getMeter(name: string, version?: string)` returning `Meter` from `@opentelemetry/api`.
4. `packages/observability/src/sdk-node.ts` exports `startNodeSdk(opts?: { serviceName?: string; otlpEndpoint?: string; resourceAttributes?: Record<string,string> }): NodeSDK` which constructs the NodeSDK with OTLP HTTP exporter (`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` env default), pg + http instrumentations, and `Resource` carrying `service.name`, `service.version`, `deployment.environment`. Honour `OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true` (no-op return).
5. `packages/observability/src/in-memory.ts` exports `createInMemoryExporter()` returning an `InMemorySpanExporter` for test assertions (per `@opentelemetry/sdk-trace-base`).
6. `apps/web/instrumentation.ts` (Next.js convention) — created or extended: `export async function register() { if (process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME==='nodejs') { const { startNodeSdk } = await import('@monopilot/observability/sdk-node'); startNodeSdk({ serviceName: 'monopilot-web' }); } }`.
7. `apps/worker/src/index.ts` modify: invoke `startNodeSdk({ serviceName: 'monopilot-worker' })` before JobRegistry construction.
8. `packages/observability/src/__tests__/otel-smoke.test.ts`: configure NodeSDK with `InMemorySpanExporter`; create a tracer; emit a span (`tracer.startActiveSpan('test.smoke', span => { span.setAttribute('foo','bar'); span.end(); })`); flush; assert the exporter received exactly one span with name `test.smoke` and attribute `foo=bar`.

## Files in scope
- packages/observability/package.json [create]
- packages/observability/tsconfig.json [create]
- packages/observability/eslint.config.mjs [create]
- packages/observability/src/index.ts [create]
- packages/observability/src/tracer.ts [create]
- packages/observability/src/meter.ts [create]
- packages/observability/src/sdk-node.ts [create]
- packages/observability/src/in-memory.ts [create]
- packages/observability/src/__tests__/otel-smoke.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/instrumentation.ts [create]
- apps/web/next.config.mjs [modify if instrumentation flag needed]
- apps/worker/src/index.ts [modify — call startNodeSdk]
- apps/worker/package.json [modify — add @monopilot/observability dep]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given otel-smoke.test.ts runs with InMemorySpanExporter wired, when tracer.startActiveSpan('test.smoke', span => {...}) invoked and flushed, then exporter received exactly one span with name 'test.smoke' and attribute foo='bar'.
2. Given apps/web/instrumentation.ts present, when Next.js boots in NEXT_RUNTIME=nodejs, then register() calls startNodeSdk({serviceName:'monopilot-web'}) (verified by vitest mock); AND Given apps/worker boots, when process starts, then startNodeSdk({serviceName:'monopilot-worker'}) called before JobRegistry construction (verified via integration test using in-memory exporter).
3. Given OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true, when startNodeSdk() invoked, then returns no-op object and registers no instrumentation.
4. Given @monopilot/observability imported, when getTracer('@monopilot/server') called, then returns Tracer whose startActiveSpan is callable.
## Test strategy
- RED: write `otel-smoke.test.ts` + a vitest mock for the `apps/web/instrumentation.ts` register() function before implementation. RED command: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/observability test && pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run __tests__/instrumentation.test.ts`.
- GREEN: implement package, exporters, sdk wiring, Next.js instrumentation, worker init.
- Run: above + `pnpm --filter @monopilot/observability typecheck`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not call `startNodeSdk()` more than once per process — wrap in idempotent guard.
- Do not set `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` to a hard-coded URL — env-gated; production endpoint set by deploy.
- Do not import `@opentelemetry/sdk-node` from `apps/web`'s client bundle — `instrumentation.ts` runs only in Node runtime, gate with `process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME==='nodejs'`.
- Do not include Sentry here — Sentry lands in T-118.
- Do not include pino here — pino lands in T-117.
- Do not log secrets via span attributes — span-attribute redaction is required (start with allowlist).
- Do not block startup if OTLP collector is unreachable — use BatchSpanProcessor with timeout, log warning, continue.

pipeline_inputs.description: Create @monopilot/observability package exporting OTel tracer + meter + startNodeSdk(); wire instrumentation.ts in apps/web; init in apps/worker; smoke test with InMemorySpanExporter asserts span export.
pipeline_inputs.details:
NodeSDK with OTLP HTTP exporter + pg + http auto-instrumentations + Resource(service.name, version, env). Idempotent startup guard. OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true short-circuits to no-op. Next.js instrumentation.ts gates on NEXT_RUNTIME=nodejs. InMemorySpanExporter exposed for test usage. Smoke test asserts span name + attribute round-trip.

AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F7 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost).
acceptance_criteria: Given otel-smoke.test.ts runs with InMemorySpanExporter wired, when tracer.startActiveSpan('test.smoke', span => {...}) invoked and flushed, then exporter received exactly one span with name 'test.smoke' and attribute foo='bar'.

Given apps/web/instrumentation.ts present, when Next.js boots in NEXT_RUNTIME=nodejs, then register() calls startNodeSdk({serviceName:'monopilot-web'}) (verified by vitest mock); AND Given apps/worker boots, when process starts, then startNodeSdk({serviceName:'monopilot-worker'}) called before JobRegistry construction (verified via integration test using in-memory exporter).
Given OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true, when startNodeSdk() invoked, then returns no-op object and registers no instrumentation.
Given @monopilot/observability imported, when getTracer('@monopilot/server') called, then returns Tracer whose startActiveSpan is callable.

GREEN: implement package + exporters + sdk wiring + Next.js instrumentation + worker init.
risk_red_lines: Do not call startNodeSdk() more than once per process — idempotent guard.
Do not import @opentelemetry/sdk-node from apps/web client bundle — gate with process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME==='nodejs'.
Do not include Sentry here — T-118 owns it.
Do not include pino here — T-117 owns it.
Do not log secrets via span attributes — allowlist-based redaction.
Do not block startup if OTLP collector unreachable — BatchSpanProcessor + warn + continue.
